description: The user interfaces in the vue application and the charts are displayed in each view
Name of the page
Text that describes the aim of the page
One or more charts displayed on a page
Visualisations that are displayed on page
A unique value that identifies a visualisation, the associated settings and data points.
The chart type (e.g., pie chart, table, bar, etc.)
A title to be displayed above a chart
A subtitle to be displayed below the title. This text provides additional context for the data points displayed in the chart.
Text to be displayed along the x-axis (horizontal axis)
A value that indicates the minimum threshold to display on the x-axis (applies only to continuous variables)
A value that indicates the minimum threshold to display on the x-axis (applies only to continuous variables)
An array of numbers that indicate the breakpoints on the x-axis
Text to be displayed along the y-axis (vertical axis)
A value that indicates the minimum threshold to display on the y-axis (applies only to continuous variables)
A value that indicates the minimum threshold to display on the y-axis (applies only to continuous variables)
An array of numbers that indicate the breakpoints on the y-axis
Color palette for distinct groups in a dataset
A number that indicates the amount of white space to add to the top of the chart
A number that indicates the amount of white space to add to the right of the chart
A number that indicates the amount of white space to add to the bottom of the chart
A number that indicates the amount of white space to add to the left of the chart
The position of the legend around the chart.
Data that is prepared to be used in a chart
A dataset is a data item that is a collection of data items.
A value that identifies this data point in a chart
Data used in a statistical visualisations (e.g., column charts, pie charts, tables, etc.)

A label to be displayed in the chart that visually identifies the data point. By default, data points must have a name and value defined. If you would like to create a chart that displays number of participants by disease and research institution, then you would put the diseases in the "name" column and the institutions in the "primary category" column.
The calculated value of the data point. A value that is described by the "name" and can be further grouped by the primary and secondary categories.

An alternative label that will be displayed in the chart in place of the actual value. This is useful if you would like to format the values in the specific format (e.g., "10.05% increase" instead of "10.05"). If you use the "value label" column, the "value" column must be populated.

A data series is a data set composed of related values displayed in a statistical graph. It is intended to use this column for line charts. This column is useful to link data points of a group in addition to the primary and secondary category columns.

time measurement is a measurement value of the duration of some interval of time or a particular instant of time (against some frame of reference). This column should be populated if you are visualising data by time. It is intended to place the value in this column (e.g., "2020,2021,2022,..." or "12, 13,14"; years, months, etc.) and set the unit in the "time unit" column.
A unit that provides context to the value in the column "time interval". E.g., "months", "years", etc.
A colour value (RGB, HEX, HSL, etc.) used to style the data point
A numerical value indicating the sorting method for data points
Dataset that contains one or more observations at a specific location
A label that identifies a location
An alternate identifier that can be used identify a location
Latitude is a geographic coordinate which refers to the angle from a point on the Earth's surface to the equatorial plane
Longitude is a geographic position that refers to the angle east or west of a reference meridian between the two geographical poles to another meridian that passes through an arbitrary point.

Content to display in the tooltip. You may write in html or as plain text. By default, the tooltip will display the name and information about the location (if defined). If this value is defined, it will replace the default tooltip.

A primary category axis is a category axis that either defines the sole value range or holds the larger set of categorical values specified by the secondary category axis. For example, if you need to group the categories listed in the "name" column, then define the grouping levels here.
A secondary category axis is a category axis that defines a finer granular part (or subset) of the value range of the primary category axis.
An alternative label that will be displayed instead of the value in "primary category" column
An alternative label that will be displayed instead of the value in "secondary category" column

A description is language entity in which elements of a language (formal or natural) are used to characterize an entity. This is useful if you are using tooltips in charts. Information placed in this column could be displayed in the charts.
A value that links one or more data points to a visualisation
Color palette for distinct groups in a dataset
A unique key that represents a categorical value in the dataset
The color code assigned to the group (HEX, RGB, etc.)
A value that links one or more data points to a visualisation
